title: selinux policy needed for systemd in containers
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

building images to boot systemd, like
```Dockerfile
FROM fedora
ENV container docker
RUN (cd /lib/systemd/system/sysinit.target.wants/; for i in *; do [ $i == \
systemd-tmpfiles-setup.service ] || rm -f $i; done); \
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/multi-user.target.wants/*;\
rm -f /etc/systemd/system/*.wants/*;\
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/local-fs.target.wants/*; \
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/sockets.target.wants/*udev*; \
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/sockets.target.wants/*initctl*; \
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/basic.target.wants/*;\
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/anaconda.target.wants/*;
VOLUME [ "/sys/fs/cgroup" ]
CMD ["/usr/sbin/init"]
```

But on a host with selinux enabled, it fails and needs some extra policy pieces

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Build that Dockerfile ^^^^

2. `sudo podman run -it --rm --systemd mysystemd`

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
```shell
$ sudo podman run -it --rm --systemd mysystemd
systemd 239 running in system mode. (+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX +IMA -APPARMOR +SMACK +SYSVINIT +UTMP +LIBCRYPTSETUP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +ACL +XZ +LZ4 +SECCOMP +BLKID +ELFUTILS +KMOD +IDN2 -IDN +
PCRE2 default-hierarchy=hybrid)
Detected virtualization docker.
Detected architecture x86-64.

Welcome to Fedora 29 (Container Image)!

Set hostname to <d5c77dd78a5b>.
Initializing machine ID from random generator.
Failed to install release agent, ignoring: No such file or directory
Failed to create /machine.slice/libpod-d5c77dd78a5bcfa2e62fc0116b49d6e162aecb45ba8725d4a4b872601c94d844.scope/init.scope control group: Permission denied
Failed to allocate manager object: Permission denied
[!!!!!!] Failed to allocate manager object, freezing.
Freezing execution.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```shell
$ sudo podman run -it --rm --systemd --name systemd systemd
systemd 239 running in system mode. (+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX +IMA -APPARMOR +SMACK +SYSVINIT +UTMP +LIBCRYPTSETUP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +ACL +XZ +LZ4 +SECCOMP +BLKID +ELFUTILS +KMOD +IDN2 -IDN +PCRE2 default-hierarchy=hybrid)
Detected virtualization docker.
Detected architecture x86-64.

Welcome to Fedora 29 (Container Image)!

Set hostname to <c2c3f2f061c8>.
Initializing machine ID from random generator.
Failed to install release agent, ignoring: No such file or directory
File /usr/lib/systemd/system/systemd-journald.service:26 configures an IP firewall (IPAddressDeny=any), but the local system does not support BPF/cgroup based firewalling.
Proceeding WITHOUT firewalling in effect! (This warning is only shown for the first loaded unit using IP firewalling.)
[  OK  ] Reached target Local File Systems.
[  OK  ] Listening on Journal Socket.
         Starting Create Volatile Files and Directories...
[  OK  ] Reached target Swap.
[  OK  ] Listening on Process Core Dump Socket.
[  OK  ] Listening on Journal Socket (/dev/log).
[  OK  ] Reached target Sockets.
[  OK  ] Reached target Slices.
[  OK  ] Reached target Paths.
         Starting Journal Service...
[  OK  ] Started Journal Service.
[  OK  ] Started Create Volatile Files and Directories.
[  OK  ] Reached target System Initialization.
[  OK  ] Started Daily Cleanup of Temporary Directories.
[  OK  ] Reached target Timers.
[  OK  ] Reached target Basic System.
[  OK  ] Reached target Multi-User System.
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:            1.1.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.5
Git Commit:         a95a49d3038462d033f84ac314ec8a3064a99cff
Built:              Tue Mar  5 13:10:31 2019
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

** work around found **
```
# ausearch -m AVC,USER_AVC,SELINUX_ERR,USER_SELINUX_ERR -i | audit2allow -m podman-systemd 

module podman-systemd 1.0;

require {
        type systemd_machined_var_lib_t;
        type cgroup_t;
        type user_home_t;
        type unlabeled_t;
        type container_t;
        class dir { add_name create read remove_name rmdir write };
        class file { open read write };
}

#============= container_t ==============

#!!!! This avc is allowed in the current policy
allow container_t cgroup_t:dir { add_name create remove_name rmdir write };

#!!!! This avc is allowed in the current policy
allow container_t cgroup_t:file write;

#!!!! This avc is allowed in the current policy
allow container_t systemd_machined_var_lib_t:dir read;

#!!!! This avc is allowed in the current policy
allow container_t unlabeled_t:dir write;

#!!!! This avc is allowed in the current policy
allow container_t user_home_t:file { open read };
# ausearch -m AVC,USER_AVC,SELINUX_ERR,USER_SELINUX_ERR -i | audit2allow -M podman-systemd 
******************** IMPORTANT ***********************
To make this policy package active, execute:

semodule -i podman-systemd.pp
# semodule -i ./podman-systemd.pp
```
now it works with selinux in enforcing mode

comments.body: Don't we have an SELinux bool to enable at least some of this?

```
man podman run 
...
       --systemd=true|false

       Run container in systemd mode. The default is true.

       If the command you running inside of the container is systemd or init, podman will setup tmpfs mount points in the following directories:

       /run, /run/lock, /tmp, /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd, /var/lib/journal

       It will also set the default stop signal to SIGRTMIN+3.

       This allow systemd to run in a confined container without any modifications.

       Note: On SELinux systems, systemd attempts to write to the cgroup file system.  Containers writing to the cgroup file system are denied by default.  The container_manage_cgroup boolean must be
       enabled for this to be allowed on an SELinux separated system.

       setsebool -P container_manage_cgroup true

```

None of this stuff is needed anymore
```
ENV container docker
RUN (cd /lib/systemd/system/sysinit.target.wants/; for i in *; do [ $i == \
systemd-tmpfiles-setup.service ] || rm -f $i; done); \
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/multi-user.target.wants/*;\
rm -f /etc/systemd/system/*.wants/*;\
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/local-fs.target.wants/*; \
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/sockets.target.wants/*udev*; \
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/sockets.target.wants/*initctl*; \
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/basic.target.wants/*;\
rm -f /lib/systemd/system/anaconda.target.wants/*;
VOLUME [ "/sys/fs/cgroup" ]
```

> None of this stuff is needed anymore

Where is that documented?

> ```
>    setsebool -P container_manage_cgroup true
> ```

completely missed this. Would make for a decent warning to check for when the `--systemd` flag is passed.
We default to --systemd true.  We could look at the command of the container and attempt give error on failure.  But these usually don't fail, they just hang.

well, i found those things in a recent example on a centos page.
So, it isn't widely known :grin:
